LAW AND MOTION 302, CROSS DEFENDANT STEVEN BRUCE'S DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED IN ITS ENTIRETY. MR. BRUCE'S REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE IS GRANTED. EVEN IF A SMALL CLAIMS JUDGMENT COULD HAVE RES JUDICATA OR COLLATERAL ESTOPPEL EFFECT, THE RECORD IS NOT SUFFICIENTLY CLEAR TO DETERMINE WHETHER THE ISSUES LITIGATED IN SMALL CLAIMS COURT ARE THE ISSUES BEING LITIGATED IN THIS CASE BEFORE THE SUPERIOR COURT. SEE PITZEN V. SUPERIOR COURT (2004) 120 CAL.APP.4TH 1374, 1381). (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN #6185.

RE-NOTICE OF MOTION FOR CONTEMPT AND SANCTIONS FILED BY PLAINTIFF BRUCE, STEVEN

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF STEVEN BRUCE'S MOTION FOR CONTEMPT AND SANCTIONS IS GRANTED, AS TO FORM INTERROGATORY NO. 14.2 AND REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION (RFP) NO. 10. DENIED AS TO RFP NO. 7. FOR FORM INTERROGATORY NO. 14.2, DEFENDANTS HAVE AGREED TO AMEND THE RESPONSE. FOR RFP NO. 7, NO FURTHER RESPONSE IS REQUIRED, DEFENDANTS AVER IN THEIR VERIFIED RESPONSE THAT ALL DOCUMENTS HAVE BEEN PRODUCED. FOR RFP NO. 10, DEFENDANTS SHALL AMEND THEIR RESPONSE AND STATE WHETHER THE DOCUMENT PRODUCED WAS REDACTED BY DEFENDANTS IN ANY WAY OR WHETHER THE DOCUMENT WAS RECEIVED BY DEFENDANTS BY A THIRD PARTY IN ITS CURRENT REDACTED FORM. WITHIN 10 DAYS, DEFENDANTS SHALL SERVE VERIFIED FURTHER RESPONSES. MONETARY SANCTIONS ARE DENIED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST GOLDSMITH, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, NOT REPORTED.

DISCOVERY 302, PLAINTIFF STEVEN BRUCE'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER ANSWERS TO INTERROGATORIES AND DEMANDS FOR PRODUCTION AND INSPECTION OF DOCUMENTS AND TO PRODUCE DOCUMENTS; AND FOR SANCTIONS IS CONTINUED TO AUGUST 30, 2013 AT 9:30 AM IN DEPT. 302. JUDGE PRO TEM: JEFFREY R. WINDSOR; CLERK L. FONG; MATTER NOT REPORTED. (302/JPT)

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF STEVEN BRUCE'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER ASNWERS TO INTERROGATORIES AND DEMANDS FOR PRODUCTION AND INSPECTION OF DOCUMENTS AND TO PRODUCE DOCUMENTS; AND FOR SANCTIONS. THE MOTION IS GRANTED, IN PART, AND DENIED, IN PART. WITHIN 20 DAYS, DEFENDANTS SHALL PROVIDE FURTHER RESPONSES AND PRODUCE RESPONSIVE DOCUMENTS, WITHOUT OBJECTIONS AS FOLLOWS: AS TO DEFENDANT CHO, GRANTED AS TO FORM INTERROGATORY NOS. 2.7, 12.4, 50.3, AND 50.4, SPECIAL INTERROGATORY NOS. 7 - 9, DEMAND FOR PRODUCTION NOS. 1 - 10 AS TO DOCUMENTS THAT HAVE NOT ALREADY BEEN PRODUCED ON BEHALF OF MAJESTIC SCEPTER, INC.; DENIED, AS TO FORM INTERROGATORY NO. 15.1, SPECIAL INTERROGATORY NO. 12. AS TO DEFENDANT KOEPKE, GRANTED AS TO FORM INTERROGATORY NOS. 12.4, 50.4, AND 50.5, SPECIAL INTERROGATORY NO. 4, DEMAND FOR PRODUCTION NOS. 1 - 10 AS TO DOCUMENTS THAT HAVE NOT ALREADY BEEN PRODUCED ON BEHALF OF MAJESTIC SCEPTER, INC.; DENIED AS TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORY NOS. 1, 2, 3, 5, AND 8. AS TO DEFENDANT MAJESTIC SCEPTER, INC., GRANTED AS TO FORM INTERROGATORY NO. 12.4, 12.6, 12.7, 14.2, 50.3, AND 50.4. AS TO THE DEMANDS FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS, PLAINTIFF IS ENTITLED TO A LEGIBLE AND VIEWABLE COPY OF THE SUBJECT PHOTOGRAPHS IDENTIFIED AS A CONTACT SIZE SHEET (MAJESTIC00048-MAJESTIC00051); THE PARTIES ARE TO MEET AND CONFER AS TO AN EFFICIENT WAY TO MAKE THOSE PHOTOGRAPHS AVAILABLE IN PRINT OR DIGITAL FORM. PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IS DENIED. THE PARTIES MUST MEET AND CONFER ON DISCOVERY MATTERS. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; NOT REPORTED.

Off Calendar Jan-16-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter. RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF STEVEN BRUCE's motion For Contempt And Sanctions is Granted, as to Form Interrogatory no. 14.2 and Request for Production (RFP) no. 10. Denied as to RFP no. 7. For Form Interrogatory no. 14.2, Defendants have agreed to amend the response. For RFP no. 7, no further response is required, Defendants aver in their verified response that all documents have been produced. For RFP no. 10, Defendants shall amend their response and state whether the document produced was redacted by Defendants in any way or whether the document was received by Defendants by a third party in its current redacted form. Within 10 days, Defendants shall serve verified further responses. Monetary sanctions are denied. Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Not reported.

Off Calendar Sep-17-2013 Continued To Dec-04-13 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Asnwers To Interrogatories And Demands For Production And Inspection Of Documents And To Produce Documents; And For Sanctions
Off Calendar Aug-30-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Steven Bruce, Esq. of the Law Office of Steven Bruce, Tel. 415.931.2314, for plaintiff and moving party;
Michael E. Zarate, Esq. of Last & Faoro, Tel. 650.696.8366, for defendant and opposing party.

RULING: A R G U E D. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted its tentative ruling on PLAINTIFF STEVEN BRUCE's Motion To Compel Further Asnwers To Interrogatories And Demands For Production And Inspection Of Documents And To Produce Documents; And For Sanctions. The motion is granted, in part, and denied, in part. Within 20 days, Defendants shall provide further responses and produce responsive documents, without objections as follows: As to Defendant Cho, granted as to Form Interrogatory nos. 2.7, 12.4, 50.3, and 50.4, Special Interrogatory nos. 7 - 9, Demand for Production nos. 1 - 10 as to documents that have not already been produced on behalf of Majestic Scepter, Inc.; denied, as to Form Interrogatory no. 15.1, Special Interrogatory no. 12. As to Defendant Koepke, granted as to Form Interrogatory nos. 12.4, 50.4, and 50.5, Special Interrogatory no. 4, Demand for Production nos. 1 - 10 as to documents that have not already been produced on behalf of Majestic Scepter, Inc.; denied as to Special Interrogatory nos. 1, 2, 3, 5, and 8. As to Defendant Majestic Scepter, Inc., granted as to Form Interrogatory no. 12.4, 12.6, 12.7, 14.2, 50.3, and 50.4. As to the demands for production of documents, Plaintiff is entitled to a legible and viewable copy of the subject photographs identified as a contact size sheet (Majestic00048-Majestic00051); the parties are to meet and confer as to an efficient way to make those photographs available in print or digital form. Plaintiff's request for sanctions is denied. The parties must meet and confer on discovery matters. Order signed. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Not Reported.

DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-13-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: STEVEN BRUCE, ESQ., 415.931.2314, CROSS DEFENDANT IN PRO PER.
MICHAEL ZARATE, ESQ,. 650.696.8350, FOR CROSS COMPLAINANTS YOUNG CHO, BERNDT KOEPKE, AND MAJESTIC SCEPTER INC.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: CROSS DEFENDANT STEVEN BRUCE'S DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT is overruled in its entirety. Mr. Bruce's request for judicial notice is granted. Even if a small claims judgment could have res judicata or collateral estoppel effect, the record is not sufficiently clear to determine whether the issues litigated in small claims court are the issues being litigated in this case before the Superior Court. See Pitzen v. Superior Court (2004) 120 Cal.App.4th 1374, 1381). (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Anthony Vaughn #6185.
